paper_title: Scenario Analysis of Soil Remediation Techniques

methods.description: SW is a water-based treatment that implements physical or chemical mechanisms for soil remediation. It involves soil particle size separation and contaminant concentration into smaller fractions, while coarse materials are decontaminated after treatment.

steps.description: SW experimental tests were conducted on sediments with particle size C using a solid-to-liquid ratio of 1:1.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct soil washing tests on sediments with specific particle size and solid-to-liquid ratio

steps.description: Analyze contaminant distribution after SW treatment to determine best and worst case scenarios.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze contaminant distribution to identify best and worst case scenarios

methods.description: LCA studies were conducted to evaluate the environmental impacts of different soil remediation scenarios, including the use of SW treatment and subsequent treatments like EK and EL.

steps.description: Conduct LCA study on sediments with particle size C considering best contaminant distribution.
steps.searchable_summary: perform LCA study on sediments with specific particle size and contaminant distribution
outputs: LCA results for best case scenario

steps.description: Conduct LCA study on sediments with particle size F considering high clay content.
steps.searchable_summary: perform LCA study on clay-rich sediments without SW treatment

steps.description: Compare each scenario with the corresponding reference scenario consisting of landfilling sediments without treatment.
steps.searchable_summary: compare LCA results with reference landfilling scenario
